claim_text: The patient has a nonhealing foot ulcer for approximately one year.
The ulcer developed after a blister that was picked at and infected.
The patient tried antibiotics previously.
The patient has Type 2 Diabetes.
The patient denies fever.
The patient denies chills.
The patient denies drainage, but then reports increased drainage.
The patient feels tired.
The patient is taking Keflex.
There is a one by two centimeter, circular wound on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot.
The wound has minimal granulation tissue.
The wound has yellow slough.
The wound has no erythema.
The wound has no cellulitis.
The wound has no fluid collection.
The wound has no necrosis.
The wound has no bony exposure.
The patient has a diabetic foot ulcer.
The patient may have neuropathy.
The patient has increased drainage.
The patient has fatigue.
An ABI will be ordered to assess blood supply.
Debridement of the ulcer is planned.
The patient will be prescribed Collagenase ointment.
The patient will be prescribed a surgical shoe to wear.
The patient will follow up in two weeks.
The patient will return in two weeks for re-evaluation and possible debridement.
